documents.extracted_text:
CATERING AUTOMATION PLATFORM
Subscription Stack Capability Reference
What we can build with what we have  —  Cursor Pro · Claude Pro · GPT Plus · Google Cloud
1. Executive Summary
This document aggregates findings from four deep research reports into a single operational reference: what the current subscription stack can actually build, where the hard limits are, and how the layers connect into a working automation platform for a catering operation.
The stack covers four distinct capability layers. No single subscription does everything — but together they form a coherent system where each tool plays to its strengths.
Layer
Tool
Primary Role
Key Constraint
Orchestration
Cursor Pro
Build environment + FTO. Scaffolds all Cloud Run services, Workflows, and integrations. Agent-drives the codebase.
API-priced model pool (~$20/mo); stay in Auto/Composer for heavy work.
Runtime
Google Cloud
Event-driven backend: Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, Firestore, Workflows, Scheduler, Tasks. The actual deployed service.
$300 free trial (90 days); $0–75/mo steady-state serverless-first.
Intelligence / Copilot
Claude Pro
Operational brain. Projects for Sales/Ops/Finance/Partners. Scheduled sweeps via Cowork. Human-in-loop judgment layer.
Session/5-hr resets; no API included; connector writes need approval.
STEM Execution
GPT Plus + Codex
High-volume technical work via Openclaw. Programmatic token throughput via OAuth — not UI-capped.
UI agent mode (deep research) is capped; API throughput is the real ceiling.
KEY INSIGHT
The critical distinction throughout: Cursor Pro is a build accelerator, not a runtime. Claude Pro is the judgment layer, not the transaction layer. GPT Plus (via Openclaw/OAuth) is a STEM workhorse with real token throughput — the 40 agent message cap applies only to deep research in the ChatGPT UI, not programmatic API usage. Google Cloud is the actual deployed backend.
2. Google Cloud (GCC) — Runtime & Infrastructure
The runtime backbone. Everything that needs to run unattended, respond to real-time events, persist data, and scale lives here. The $300 free trial covers the build period; steady-state for a small/medium catering operation should land between $0–$75/month on a serverless-first architecture.
2.1 Core Architecture Pattern
The right architecture is serverless-first and event-driven — matching catering's bursty workload (peak around events, quiet otherwise):
Cloud Run — primary API server, webhook receiver, event handlers. Scales to zero between events.
Firestore — operational system of record (events, orders, menus, staff, inventory).
Pub/Sub — event bus decoupling all services (order.created, invoice.sent, inventory.low).
Workflows — multi-step process orchestration with durable retries and error handling.
Cloud Scheduler — cron triggers for recurring reports, nightly syncs, weekly labor runs.
Cloud Tasks — retryable side-effects queue (email sends, external CRM updates).
Secret Manager — API keys, webhook signing secrets, DB credentials.
Cloud Storage — contracts, BEOs, invoices, photos, generated PDFs.
BigQuery — analytics layer for lead attribution, food cost trends, labor reporting.
2.2 What's in the Free Tier (Always-Free Monthly Limits)
Service
Free Tier
Verdict
Cloud Run
2M requests, 180k vCPU-seconds, 360k GiB-seconds/mo
✅ Free at small scale
Firestore
1 GiB storage, 50k reads/day, 20k writes/day, 10 GiB outbound/mo
✅ Free at small scale
Pub/Sub
First 10 GiB throughput/month
✅ Free at small scale
Workflows
5,000 internal steps + 2,000 external HTTP calls/month
✅ Free at small scale
Cloud Tasks
First 1M operations/month
✅ Free at small scale
Cloud Scheduler
~3 jobs/month free; $0.10/job/31 days beyond
✅ Pennies
Vision API (OCR)
1,000 units/month free
✅ Free for receipts/BEOs
Speech-to-Text
60 minutes/month free
✅ Free for voicemail transcription
Maps/Routes/Geocoding
~10k free calls/SKU/month (Essentials tier)
✅ Free for delivery routing
Cloud Storage
5 GiB free; lifecycle rules control cost
✅ Free for early volumes
GKE
$0.10/cluster-hour baseline (~$73/mo)
⚡ Avoid — not worth it
Memorystore (Redis)
Expensive even at small sizes
⚡ Avoid — not worth it
Cloud SQL
$0.0105/hr shared-core; easy to overspend 24/7
🔧 Add later if needed
2.3 Workflow-to-Service Mapping
Workflow
GCP Services
Minimal Viable Implementation
Order / Lead Intake
Cloud Run + Firestore + Pub/Sub + Workflows
One /orders endpoint → publishes order.created → Workflow creates calendar event, prep plan, confirmation email
Calendar Sync
Workflows + Calendar API + Cloud Scheduler
Calendar is the operational source of truth; push notifications + nightly sync job
Staffing / Scheduling
Workflows + Sheets API + Calendar API
Workflow writes staffing rows to Sheets and creates Calendar blocks for shifts
Procurement / Shopping Lists
Pub/Sub + Workflows + Cloud Tasks + Gmail/Sheets APIs
inventory.low event triggers list generation; pushes to shared Sheet; emails vendor draft POs
Invoicing / Billing
Cloud Run + Firestore + Cloud Storage + Gmail API
Generate PDF invoices, store in GCS, send via Gmail API, update payment status
Reimbursements
Cloud Run + Vision API + Firestore
OCR receipts → extract fields → write reimbursement entries
Weekly Labor Report
Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Run + Sheets API
Nightly timesheet aggregation; scheduled weekly report generation
Close CRM Integration
Cloud Run webhook + Pub/Sub + Firestore
Webhook receiver for Close events → pipeline state in Firestore → downstream automation
Venue / Referral Management
Firestore + Scheduler + Workflows
Rep-assigned venue entities; scheduled check-in reminders; optional Places API enrichment
Notifications
Cloud Tasks + Gmail API
Retryable email queue; SMS via Twilio if needed
Analytics / Reporting
BigQuery + Firestore export
Lead source attribution, food cost %, labor %, conversion funnel
2.4 Cost Traps to Avoid
⚠ AVOID
GKE has a $72–73/month baseline cluster fee regardless of workload. Memorystore is expensive even at small sizes. Cloud SQL left running 24/7 accumulates quickly. Always-on resources are the enemy of a bursty catering workload — go serverless-first.
$300 CREDIT STRATEGY
Use the $300 trial credit for controlled spikes: data migration, Document AI experiments for BEO parsing, Route Optimization API testing. Protect it from always-on resources — those should stay inside free tier limits.
3. Cursor Pro — Orchestration & Build Environment
Cursor is the File Tree Owner (FTO) and primary build environment. The key distinction: Cursor Pro is an engineering multiplier — it builds and maintains everything in the stack faster, but it does not replace any of the runtime infrastructure.
CORRECT FRAMING
Cursor can build essentially the full product surface (integrations, Cloud Run services, Workflows definitions, admin dashboards, webhook handlers). But 'how much can be implemented using Cursor Pro alone' is low as a deployed service — it's the forge, not the product.
3.1 What Cursor Pro Includes
Unlimited Tab + Auto/Composer model usage (the everyday workhorse)
~$20/month pool of API-priced frontier model usage (Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o, etc.)
MCP (Model Context Protocol) — connect external systems and data sources into the IDE
Cloud Agents — remote isolated machines that clone repos, run tests, push changes
Rules / Hooks / Skills — customizable automation building blocks for workflow enforcement
Cloud Agents API — programmatic agent invocation with rate limits (300 req/min)
3.2 How It Maps to This Stack
Capability
How Cursor Handles It
Status
Cloud Run service scaffolding
Agent generates endpoints, handlers, tests
✅ Core use case — stays in Auto/Composer
Webhook handler generation
Close, Calendar, Stripe signature verification patterns
✅ Core use case
Workflows definitions
YAML/JSON orchestration definitions with error handling
✅ Accelerates significantly
Integration modules
Close API, Sheets API, Calendar API client code
✅ Core use case
Admin dashboard UI
Internal ops surfaces for event management
✅ Core use case
Test suite generation
Unit + integration tests for all services
✅ Core use case
MCP-connected dev
Real-time data from staging systems into IDE context
🔧 Requires MCP server setup
Long-horizon agentic builds
Multi-step scaffolding of entire modules
⚡ Burns API-priced pool — use sparingly
Production runtime
Deployed service that runs the catering platform
⚡ Not Cursor's role — that's GCC
3.3 Usage Model & Constraints
Two usage pools: (1) Auto+Composer — generous, everyday use; (2) API-priced frontier models — ~$20/mo base, expandable
For this stack: stay in Auto/Composer for routine scaffolding; reserve API-priced usage for complex multi-step agent runs
Privacy Mode should be enabled — zero data retention with model providers; no PCI-regulated data in prompts ever
Cloud Agents API is the right hook for programmatic Cursor invocation from Openclaw or scheduled triggers
DATA SAFETY
Cursor's Terms prohibit PCI DSS-regulated data in prompts. Use redacted fixtures and synthetic data for debugging payment flows. Real cardholder data never enters the IDE context.
4. Claude Pro — Intelligence Layer & Copilot
Claude Pro is the judgment and intelligence layer — the 'semi' in semi-autonomous. It handles human-in-the-loop operations, scheduled document sweeps, proposal generation, reporting, and operational copiloting across all Google Workspace content.
WHAT IT IS NOT
Claude Pro is a paid consumer chat product, not an application platform. It does not include API/Console access (that's a separate product). It cannot reliably run event-driven, transactional, auditable automation without GCC underneath it.
4.1 Capability Surfaces Available
Projects — self-contained workspaces with knowledge bases + instructions; scale via RAG at context limits
Connectors — Google Workspace (Gmail draft, Calendar read/write, Drive search/read), Slack, ClickUp, Close (via remote MCP)
Claude Code (cloud) — scheduled tasks that run as cloud-executed jobs; polling-based automation
Cowork (desktop) — scheduled recurring sweeps; tree sweeps against Drive/Calendar content
200K context window — large enough to process full BEOs, weekly ops packs, pipeline exports
4.2 Feature Feasibility for Catering Operations
Feature
Feasibility
Weekly ops briefing (calendar + tasks)
✅ Fully feasible — Cowork scheduled sweep against Calendar + Drive
Proposal / menu draft generation
✅ Fully feasible — Projects with menu knowledge base + templates
Pipeline forecast narrative
✅ Fully feasible — analyze Sheets export, generate forecast with scenarios
Reimbursement extraction from receipts
✅ Fully feasible — image/PDF input → structured CSV output
Venue outreach draft generation
✅ Fully feasible — batch email drafts from venue contact list
Labor report analysis + anomaly flags
✅ Fully feasible — timesheet CSV → narrative report + anomaly table
Close CRM updates (autonomous)
⚡ Partial — can generate 'what to update' payloads; write-back needs API glue
Event-driven automation (webhooks)
⚡ Not feasible in Pro — needs GCC runtime underneath
Calendar event creation (unattended)
⚡ Partial — connector supports it but requires user approval in chat
Multi-user governance / audit trail
⚡ Not feasible — consumer tier data retention; no ZDR on Pro
Scheduled weekly cash visibility report
✅ Feasible via Claude Code scheduled task + Sheets connector
BEO → checklist / prep plan extraction
✅ Feasible — large context handles full BEO documents
4.3 Recommended Project Structure
Organize Claude Pro around four persistent Projects, each with its own knowledge base and system instructions:
Sales Project — pipeline templates, proposal formats, lead source taxonomy, Close CRM context
Ops Project — BEO formats, event execution checklists, staffing schemas, venue SOP library
Finance Project — cash visibility templates, labor report schemas, reimbursement policy, weekly CFO brief format
Partners Project — venue/planner contact list, outreach cadence rules, relationship notes, referral attribution
4.4 Connector Limitations to Know
Gmail: can draft but cannot send — human approval required for outbound email
Drive: text extraction only — images embedded in Docs/Slides are not processed by connector
Drive: Sheets accessed via CSV export; xlsx→Sheets conversion not supported
Calendar: create/update/delete supported, but unattended write actions require user approval in chat
Close CRM: no first-party connector — use remote MCP server (available on Pro) as the workaround
5. GPT Plus + Codex (via Openclaw) — STEM Execution Layer
Codex inside Openclaw is the high-volume technical execution layer. The correct mental model: when running programmatically via OAuth through Openclaw, you are operating against API rate limits and token throughput — not the consumer UI caps that the GPT Plus research report focused on.
IMPORTANT CORRECTION
The 40 agent message/month cap referenced in the research applies specifically to deep research mode in the ChatGPT UI. Programmatic Codex usage via Openclaw/OAuth operates on API token throughput, which is significantly more generous and scales with spend tier.
5.1 Why Codex in Openclaw
GPT Plus is generous with token usage via OAuth — best token-per-dollar of any layer in the stack
Openclaw provides cron heartbeats, subagent orchestration, remote invocation, and a GUI surface
Keeping Openclaw as gateway (not FTO) avoids file tree pollution from agent artifacts
Codex is the strongest STEM/technical reasoning agent — routes the right work to the right model
5.2 What to Route to Codex
Task Type
Examples
Routing
Complex calculations
Recipe scaling math, food cost optimization, labor allocation
✅ Route to Codex
Code generation (technical)
Algorithms, optimization solvers, data transformation scripts
✅ Route to Codex
Structured data extraction
BEO parsing, invoice field extraction, timesheet normalization
✅ Route to Codex
Event complexity scoring
Multi-factor scoring rubrics from historical event data
✅ Route to Codex
Staffing optimization
Constraint-based assignment across multiple events/locations
✅ Route to Codex
Inventory forecasting
Demand prediction from upcoming event schedule
✅ Route to Codex
Proposal language / tone
Client-facing communication, relationship nuance
🔗 Route to Claude
Operational judgment calls
Ambiguous situations requiring context + empathy
🔗 Route to Claude
SOP documentation
Process writing, training materials, SOW language
🔗 Route to Claude
5.3 Openclaw Architecture Role
Cron heartbeats fire scheduled jobs → triggers Cloud Run endpoints in GCC via webhook
Subagents handle parallel technical workloads without burning Claude's quota
Remote GUI gives visibility into agent runs without needing to be in Cursor
Openclaw directory stays clean — it's a gateway, not a file home base
FILE TREE DISCIPLINE
The research correctly flags that using Openclaw's directory as the main file tree leads to agent artifacts polluting working directories. Cursor owns the file tree. Openclaw is a gateway that fires into GCC and routes work to agents. These roles do not overlap.
6. How the Layers Connect — Handoff Architecture
This is the answer to the core orchestration question: when a trigger fires, where does the work land?
6.1 The Event Flow
A canonical end-to-end flow through the stack:
Trigger fires (cron from Openclaw, webhook from Close, form submit, calendar change)
Hits a Cloud Run endpoint in GCC — validates, writes to Firestore, publishes Pub/Sub event
Pub/Sub triggers Workflows orchestration for the job type
Workflows routes to downstream services:
→ Intelligence tasks (drafts, analysis, summaries) → Claude API call
→ STEM/technical tasks (calculations, parsing, optimization) → Codex via Openclaw
→ Google Workspace writes (Calendar, Sheets, Gmail draft) → direct API calls
→ Retryable side-effects (email send, CRM update) → Cloud Tasks queue
Results write back to Firestore; Slack notification fires on completion
Cowork scheduled sweeps run independently against Drive/Calendar; produce digests into Projects
6.2 Routing Logic by Task Type
Task
Where It Lands
AI Layer
New lead from Close webhook
GCC (Cloud Run → Pub/Sub → Workflows)
Claude: draft follow-up proposal
Weekly ops briefing
Cowork scheduled sweep
Claude: generate digest from Calendar + Drive
BEO received → event setup
GCC (Workflows)
Codex: extract structured fields; Claude: generate checklist
Prep list generation
GCC (Cloud Scheduler → Workflows)
Codex: scale recipes; GCC: push to Sheets
Labor report (weekly)
GCC (Cloud Scheduler)
Claude: analyze timesheet CSV, flag anomalies
Receipt reimbursement
GCC (Vision API) + Claude
Claude: structured extraction + categorization
Staffing optimization
GCC (Workflows)
Codex: constraint solver; GCC: write to Calendar/Sheets
Venue outreach cadence
Cowork or GCC (Scheduler)
Claude: generate email drafts per venue rep
Invoice generation
GCC (Cloud Run → Cloud Storage → Gmail)
GCC: PDF generation and send
Pipeline forecast
Claude Pro (scheduled task)
Claude: narrative forecast from Sheets export
6.3 Priority Build Sequence
Based on the research, this is the right order of operations:
Phase 1 — Stabilize knowledge: Build four Claude Pro Projects (Sales/Ops/Finance/Partners). Standardize artifact formats (event brief, shopping list, labor report, weekly pack).
Phase 2 — Automate recurring reporting: Deploy Cloud Scheduler + Claude Code scheduled tasks for daily calendar digest and weekly ops pack. Harden Workspace connectors.
Phase 3 — Integration backbone: Build Close CRM webhook receiver (Cloud Run) → Pub/Sub pipeline. This is the highest-leverage first GCC integration since it drives the most downstream automation.
Phase 4 — Full orchestration: Wire Workflows to Claude API + Codex for intelligent task routing. Add MCP connectors for remaining SaaS tools.
Phase 5 — Customer-facing (if needed): Menus + ordering + payments. Keep PCI scope minimal with hosted payment provider approach.
7. Known Limitations & Gaps
7.1 Things No Subscription Covers Out of the Box
Event-driven automation (webhooks) — requires GCC runtime; not available in Claude Pro or GPT Plus consumer tiers
Zero Data Retention (ZDR) — commercial API arrangement only; consumer Pro tiers have 30-day retention by default (5-year if model improvement is on)
Multi-user governance / audit trail — neither Claude Pro nor GPT Plus provide this; needs GCC + proper logging
Native Anthropic embeddings — Anthropic does not offer an embedding model; use Voyage AI or another provider for RAG over menus/BEOs/SOPs
Transactional payment processing — PCI scope requires a hosted/embedded payments provider (Stripe recommended); never pass cardholder data through any AI prompt
7.2 Close CRM is the First Priority Integration
All four research reports flag Close CRM as a pain point: no first-party connector on Claude's side, no native GPT Plus integration, requires direct API or Zapier glue. Build the Close webhook → Cloud Run → Pub/Sub pipeline first. Everything downstream depends on having pipeline state in the system.
7.3 Drive Connector Image Limitation
If critical documents (receipts, BEOs, venue contracts) live in Drive as images embedded in Docs or Slides, the Drive connector will not process those images. Route those through Vision API in GCC or submit images directly to Claude for extraction. Standardize document formats to avoid this where possible.
7.4 Openclaw Maturity
Openclaw's cron/subagent/remote functionality is described as early-stage. Keeping it as a gateway rather than a load-bearing platform is the right call until the product matures. The architecture is designed to not depend on Openclaw's stability for core operations — GCC handles the reliable runtime layer.
8. Quick Reference — What to Use for What
I need to...
Use This
Layer
I need to build a new Cloud Run service
Cursor Pro (Auto/Composer)
Build
I need a proposal drafted for a lead
Claude Pro (Sales Project)
Intelligence
I need to process 500 receipt images
GCC (Vision API) + Claude extract
Runtime + Intelligence
I need to scale a recipe for 300 guests
Codex via Openclaw
STEM
I need a weekly ops briefing at 7am Monday
Cowork scheduled sweep
Copilot
I need a Close webhook to trigger downstream work
Cloud Run + Pub/Sub (GCC)
Runtime
I need staffing optimization across 4 events
Codex via Openclaw
STEM
I need to generate venue outreach emails in bulk
Claude Pro (Partners Project)
Intelligence
I need invoices auto-generated and emailed
GCC (Workflows + Cloud Tasks + Gmail API)
Runtime
I need to analyze labor cost trends over 6 months
BigQuery + Claude analysis
Runtime + Intelligence
I need a new MCP connector for a SaaS tool
Cursor Pro (build) → Claude Pro (use)
Build → Copilot
I need a cron job that runs every day at 6am
Cloud Scheduler (GCC) or Openclaw heartbeat
Runtime
Legend: ✅ Ready to use  ⚡ Has constraints  🔧 Requires setup first  🔗 Route elsewhere
documents.summary: CATERING AUTOMATION PLATFORM Subscription Stack Capability Reference What we can build with what we have  —  Cursor Pro · Claude Pro · GPT Plus · Google Cloud 1. Executive Summary This document aggreg

Document,Table,Topic,Primary capability / role,Outcome / level,Constraints / dependencies / cost,Action / implementation note
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Serverless compute - Cloud Run,Primary API + webhook receiver + UI backend + event handlers; scale-to-zero ideal for bursty operations,Service,"Request-based free tier includes 2M requests, 180k vCPU-seconds, 360k GiB-seconds per month (billing-account scoped), then billed per vCPU-second / GiB-second and per million requests.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Functions - Cloud Functions (Cloud Run functions),"Lightweight glue code: webhook endpoints, Pub/Sub handlers, scheduled jobs",Service,"Use for “small, single-purpose” handlers; costs align closely with Cloud Run request-based pricing patterns.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Orchestration - Workflows,Orchestrate multi-step processes (order -> calendar -> tasks -> email -> invoice); durable retries & error handling,Service,"Always-free: 5,000 internal steps + 2,000 external HTTP calls monthly; then $0.01/1k internal steps, $0.025/1k external steps.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Eventing - Pub/Sub,"Event bus for decoupling: order.created, invoice.sent, inventory.low",Service,"Always-free: first 10 GiB throughput per month; then throughput billed (e.g., $40/TiB in standard regions).",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Scheduling - Cloud Scheduler,"Cron-like triggers: daily inventory checks, weekly labor report, nightly sync",Service,Billed per job definition (not per execution): $0.10/job/31 days; there is a small free allowance (commonly cited as first 3 jobs/month).,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Background queue - Cloud Tasks,"Reliable retries for side effects: send emails/SMS, generate PDFs, write to external systems",Service,"First 1M operations/month free, then $0.40 per million operations (chunked).",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,App platform - App Engine,Alternative to Cloud Run for some web apps; can host internal dashboards,Service,"App Engine Standard has Free Tier instance-hour allowances (e.g., daily free quotas for certain instance classes) but can be less flexible than Cloud Run for modern container workflows.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Managed VMs - Compute Engine,"“Managed VM” approach: run a monolith, cron jobs, legacy integrations",Service,"Free tier includes one e2-micro VM plus limited disk/egress in specific US regions; beyond that, VM costs + disk + networking apply.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Containers / microservices - GKE,"Kubernetes-based microservices, advanced networking/policies, multi-service ops",Service,Has a cluster management fee of $0.10/cluster-hour (≈$72-$73/month baseline) plus node compute/storage.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Storage - Cloud Storage,"Store contracts, BEOs, menus, invoices, photos, logs exports",Service,Charged by storage class + operations + egress; supports bucket-level location choice and features like object versioning / lifecycle policies for cost control.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Database (NoSQL) - Firestore,Operational system-of-record for events/orders/menus/tasks; flexible for rapid iteration,Service,"One free database; free tier includes 1 GiB storage, 50k reads/day, 20k writes/day, 10 GiB outbound/month. PITR/backups are billable features.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Database (SQL) - Cloud SQL,"Relational back office (invoices, line items, inventory ledger) when strict SQL constraints needed",Service,"Shared-core pricing starts around $0.0105/hour for db-f1-micro (plus storage, backups, networking); PITR and automated backups available.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Cache - Memorystore,"Cache hot data (menus, route lookups), rate-limit tokens, session store",Service,Often expensive for small budgets (example pricing shows large monthly costs even at modest sizes). Avoid unless you have clear performance need.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Analytics - BigQuery,"Reporting: lead sources, conversion, food cost, labor %, vendor spend, delivery SLA; billing analytics",Service,BigQuery has a free tier and free operations; you can also export Cloud Billing data into BigQuery for cost governance.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Security - IAM,"Roles, service accounts, least-privilege service-to-service auth",Service,Core to controlling who can invoke services / access data; service account best practices are explicitly documented.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Secrets - Secret Manager,"Store API keys (CRM, vendors, SMS), DB creds, signing keys",Service,6 secret versions free; access operations charged after free tier; Cloud Run can be configured to access secrets via IAM role bindings.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Networking - VPC,"Private networking, egress control, private service access (e.g., private DB)",Service,"VPC itself is foundational; costs typically appear via egress, NAT, load balancing, etc. Networking pricing applies during and after free trial (charged against credits in trial).",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Observability - Cloud Monitoring (Observability),"Monitoring, alerting, uptime checks, dashboards",Service,"Some Monitoring features have free allotments (e.g., 1M uptime check executions/month), then metered charges.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Logging (related) - Cloud Logging,Operational audit trail; troubleshooting workflows and order flows,Service,"Log ingestion has a free tier commonly referenced as first 50 GiB/month, then ~$0.50/GiB ingestion; retention beyond 30 days billed at $0.01/GiB-month. Manage via exclusions/retention.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Billing governance - Cloud Billing API + Budgets,Automate guardrails and reporting; programmatic budget notifications to Pub/Sub,Service,Budgets can trigger Pub/Sub notifications; Billing export to BigQuery supports cost analytics.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,Maps - Maps/Places/Routes APIs,"Delivery routing, geocoding venues, address validation, ETA estimates",Service,"Since March 1, 2025: pricing uses free monthly calls per SKU (Essentials commonly 10k free calls/SKU). Route compute and geocoding have explicit caps and per-1,000-event pricing.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,AI/ML - Vision API,"Receipt/photo scanning, label/OCR, basic image understanding",Service,"Free tier: 1,000 units/month; quickstarts explicitly note the free allowance.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,AI/ML - Speech-to-Text,Voice-based order capture; transcribe voicemails,Service,Free tier: 60 minutes/month.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,AI/ML - Document AI,"Parse invoices, contracts, banquet event orders (BEOs), W-9s; structured extraction",Service,Paid per processing; positioned as “try free” via credits for new customers; use sparingly unless you have volume economics.,
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,IoT - IoT (note: IoT Core retired),Temperature logger ingestion + alerts; cold chain compliance,Service,"IoT Core was retired Aug 16, 2023; implement IoT ingestion using generic endpoints (Cloud Run + Pub/Sub) or partner platforms.",
Google Cloud Credits,Service inventory,CI/CD (recommended) - Cloud Build + Artifact Registry + Cloud Deploy,Build/deploy Cloud Run services reliably,Service,Cloud Build has free build-minutes allowances (documented in Free Tier tables); Artifact Registry has a small free storage tier; Cloud Deploy pricing includes a free first active multi-target pipeline and $5/month for additional multi-target pipelines.,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,"Order intake (web, email, form, CRM lead)","Start with one Cloud Run endpoint /orders that validates, writes an Order doc, and publishes order.created. A Workflows execution then creates/updates: a calendar event, prep plan, and customer confirmation email.",Triggers: Web form submit; inbound email; CRM webhook,Cloud Run (HTTP) + Firestore + Pub/Sub + Workflows,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Menu management,"Implement menu items as Firestore documents. For adoption, add a “Sheets sync” job (read/write via Sheets API) so the team can keep using familiar sheets during transition.",Triggers: Internal admin edits; seasonal updates,Firestore + Cloud Run admin UI; optionally Sheets as UI surface,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Inventory tracking,Track inventory deltas per event. Nightly job computes “projected stock” and flags low items. Export snapshots to BigQuery later for analysis (food cost trends).,Triggers: After event closeout; scheduled nightly reconciliation,Firestore + Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Run job + BigQuery (optional),
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Procurement / shopping lists,Generate a purchase list (by vendor/category) and push to a shared Sheet; email vendors automatically with a draft PO (PDF stored in Cloud Storage). Use Cloud Tasks for retries.,Triggers: inventory.low event; weekly planning,Pub/Sub + Workflows + Cloud Tasks + Gmail/Sheets APIs,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Recipe scaling,"Implement a deterministic scaling service: input (recipe_id, servings, dietary constraints) -> output scaled ingredient quantities. Store results per event as an immutable “prep plan” version.",Triggers: Order final headcount changes,Cloud Run job/function + Firestore,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Scheduling / staffing,"Workflow maintains a canonical event schedule in Firestore, then writes “staffing needs” rows to Sheets and creates Calendar blocks for shifts. The extraction emphasizes calendar-driven planning and labor allocation pressure.",Triggers: New event confirmed; weekly labor planning,Workflows + Calendar API + Sheets API,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Delivery routing,"MVI: geocode/validate addresses, compute a route, store ETAs; share route link to driver. Keep within free SKU caps first (Essentials often 10k free calls).",Triggers: Delivery window set; address confirmed,Cloud Run + Routes API + (optional) Route Optimization API,

SMS can be added later via an external provider.,Triggers: Order confirmation; reminders; delivery updates,"Cloud Tasks + Gmail API; optional SMS via entity[""company"",""Twilio"",""sms platform""]",
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Billing / invoicing,"MVI: Generate invoice PDFs, store in Cloud Storage, email via Gmail API, update payment status in Firestore. If you need relational accounting rigor, introduce Cloud SQL later; pricing starts at small shared-core rates but it’s easy to overspend if left running 24/7.",Triggers: Event completion; deposit due; balance due,Cloud Run + Firestore/Cloud SQL + Cloud Storage + Gmail API,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Compliance / temperature logging,"Since IoT Core is retired, ingest device telemetry via HTTPS/MQTT endpoints you operate (Cloud Run) then store in Firestore and alert on thresholds. Retain logs per policy; add exports for audits.",Triggers: Periodic sensor reads; manual checklists,Cloud Run ingestion + Pub/Sub + Firestore; IoT Core replacement approach,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Feedback / CRM,Record feedback responses; compute NPS-like metrics; push summary to analytics. Automate “request review” emails 24-72 hours after event completion.,Triggers: Post-event survey; review request,Cloud Run + Firestore + BigQuery + Gmail API,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Lead source tracking (from extraction),Store lead source and stage transitions as events for later attribution analysis. Use BigQuery for funnel reporting once volumes grow.,Triggers: New lead; deal stage changes,Cloud Run webhook + Pub/Sub + BigQuery,
Google Cloud Credits,Workflow mapping,Venue / referral relationship management (from extraction),Maintain venue/contact entities with assigned “owner” rep; scheduled reminders create tasks and emails. Optionally enrich addresses via Places/Geocoding.,Triggers: Quarterly check-ins; rep assignments,Firestore + Scheduler + Workflows + Maps Places (optional),
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Cloud Run,"vCPU-seconds, GiB-seconds, and requests beyond free tier",Cost driver,"Often $0 if inside free tier (2M req + 180k vCPU-sec + 360k GiB-sec). Above that, request-based billing applies.",
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Firestore,reads/writes/storage beyond free allowance; paid features like PITR/backups,Cost driver,Often $0 at modest scale if you stay under free daily quotas; beware that PITR/backups are billable features.,
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Pub/Sub,throughput beyond 10 GiB/month,Cost driver,Often $0; spikes are usually still small unless streaming large payloads.,
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Workflows,steps beyond free,Cost driver,"Typically pennies to a few dollars; free: 5,000 internal + 2,000 external monthly.",
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Cloud Tasks,operations beyond 1M/month,Cost driver,Usually $0 in early phases; first 1M operations free.,
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Cloud Scheduler,job definitions beyond small free allowance,Cost driver,Low single digits per month unless you create many schedules ($0.10/job/31 days).,
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Maps (Routes/Places/Geocoding),"per-1,000 event pricing beyond free SKU caps",Cost driver,"Often $0 initially because Essentials commonly include 10k free calls/SKU; route compute is $5 per 1,000 after free cap in Essentials.",
Google Cloud Credits,Cost model,Logging,ingestion beyond free amounts + retention beyond 30 days,Cost driver,"Can become material; first ~50 GiB/month often free, then about $0.50/GiB; retention >30 days $0.01/GiB-month.",
Google Cloud Credits,Implementation options,Serverless-first (recommended),Cloud Run + Firestore + Workflows + Pub/Sub + Tasks/Scheduler,High; add services and load without re-platforming,Pros: Lowest ops burden; scale-to-zero; aligns with bursty catering workflows; cost largely proportional to usage | Cons: Requires careful event/idempotency design; local dev patterns differ from VM-style “always on” | Est. monthly cost: $0-$75 typical if staying in free tiers and controlling logs; spikes if Maps/AI heavy,
Google Cloud Credits,Implementation options,Containerized microservices,Kubernetes (GKE) + multiple services; internal service mesh/network controls,"Very high, but only worth it when complexity/traffic justify",Pros: Strong for complex multi-service platforms; rich policy controls; standard microservice tooling | Cons: Baseline cluster fee plus nodes; higher SRE burden and tuning; usually poor fit for $300-credit MVP | Est. monthly cost: $150-$500+ depending on nodes; baseline includes $0.10/cluster-hour (~$72-$73/month) plus compute/storage,
Google Cloud Credits,Implementation options,Managed VMs,Compute Engine VM(s) running monolith + cron; optional Cloud SQL,Medium; scales but at growing ops complexity,Pros: Familiar ops model; can be cheap if using free e2-micro tier and very small workload | Cons: Patch/ops burden; scaling is manual; uptime costs when left running; risk of “accidental always-on” spend | Est. monthly cost: $0-$200 depending on VM sizing and whether you run Cloud SQL 24/7; free tier exists for e2-micro in certain regions,

GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Overall business shape,"Use Projects + custom GPTs to document SOPs, roles, and operational artifacts; generate process maps and playbooks.",Partially feasible,"“Documentation” is feasible; “automation” still needs triggers + data plumbing. | Inputs: Existing SOP docs, sample events, existing spreadsheet templates.","“Process mining prompt”: ask for workflow, produce swimlanes + checklists + RACI."
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Sales and lead management,"Draft responses, proposals, qualification scripts; analyze lead notes; use email/calendar apps for context.",Partially feasible,No reliable end-to-end “new lead -> CRM update -> follow-up sequence” without external automation (and/or CRM app availability). | Inputs: CRM export CSV; email threads; proposal templates in Drive.,“Triage -> schema -> draft”: extract lead fields to JSON then generate email/proposal.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Lead source awareness matters a lot,"Data analysis on lead source fields for conversion, close time, and revenue attribution.",Partially feasible,Requires consistently captured data; may require ETL from CRM into a sheet. | Inputs: CSV export from CRM; standardized “source” taxonomy.,“Analytics prompt”: compute funnel by source; return charts + narrative + actions.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Close CRM is important,"GPT Plus can advise and generate updates, but cannot natively act in Close unless there is an app or you use GPT Actions (which requires an external API endpoint).",Not feasible,Missing native Close app + no Plus-as-backend. Practical automation requires Zapier/API glue. | Inputs: Close API / Zapier sync into Sheets; or migrate to a CRM with a ChatGPT app.,“CRM delta prompt”: produce “what to update” payloads for manual entry or downstream automation.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Lead tracker forecasts incoming money,GPT can analyze pipeline tracker sheets and generate weekly forecast narratives and scenarios.,Partially feasible,Scheduled automation is limited (Tasks can’t use file uploads or GPTs). | Inputs: Pipeline sheet; assumptions for probability and expected close date.,“Forecast with assumptions”: compute base/upside/downside and confidence notes.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Weekly labor reporting,"Upload timesheets/payroll exports; compute labor cost per event, per role, per worker; generate a weekly labor report.",Partially feasible,Data capture quality; no guaranteed writeback into payroll/HR. | Inputs: Timesheet export; event roster; pay rates table.,“Labor report prompt”: enforce schema; output summary + anomalies list.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Scheduling is a major operational function,Use calendar apps to summarize upcoming events and detect conflicts; generate staffing plans and run-of-show. (Calendar apps may be read-only depending on provider/app.),Partially feasible,Creating/modifying calendar events is not reliably supported across plans/providers; agent mode is capped. | Inputs: Calendar events; staffing availability; travel-time heuristics.,“Schedule digest prompt”: produce daily/weekly operational brief + staffing needs.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Calendar is a core source of operational truth,Projects can reference Drive/Slack links; apps can search calendar/email for context.,Partially feasible,“Truth source” still needs normalization and structured extraction. | Inputs: Calendar + linked BEO/Docs; standardized naming conventions.,“Calendar -> ops schema”: extract event objects into normalized JSON rows.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Jobs/events are operationally detailed,"GPT excels at converting complex event text (BEOs, emails) into structured checklists, timelines, staffing plans.",Partially feasible,"If BEO is PDF/image, extraction quality varies; tasks can’t use file uploads; human review required. | Inputs: BEO PDF; venue docs; equipment inventory list.",“Extract -> validate -> generate”: 3-pass approach with explicit validation rules.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Visual readability of operations,GPT can propose dashboard layouts and generate “one-page ops brief” formats; can generate tables/charts from sheets.,Partially feasible,Cannot guarantee UI rendering in Google Sheets UI; you still implement formatting. | Inputs: Ops sheet; color conventions; column schema.,“Dashboard spec prompt”: output a layout spec + formulas + conditional formatting rules.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Some jobs are more complex than others,Build a complexity scoring rubric; use GPT to classify events and suggest staffing/equipment.,Partially feasible,Needs historical outcomes to calibrate; risk of hallucinated constraints without data. | Inputs: Past events dataset (event attributes + staff hours + issues).,“Scoring rubric prompt”: produce score + reason codes + resource plan.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Reimbursements / expense handling exists,"GPT can extract receipt fields (amount, category, event) from uploads and produce reimbursement entries; create summaries.",Partially feasible,Must avoid cardholder data; cannot automate payments; needs accounting system integration for full flow. | Inputs: Receipts; chart of accounts; reimbursement policy doc.,“Receipt extraction”: output a strict CSV row set + confidence markers.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Financial visibility is weekly and practical,GPT can synthesize cash outlook reports from spreadsheet data and narrative assumptions with scenario analysis.,Partially feasible,Requires good data hygiene; true automation needs scheduled ingestion from bank/processor (not Plus). | Inputs: Weekly cash sheet; expected withdrawals/payroll; vendor bills.,“Weekly CFO brief”: table + risks + recommended moves next 7/14 days.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Shared food shopping / prep list,"GPT can generate scaled shopping lists, prep sequencing, and batch plans from menus and headcount; can output into sheets/docs if the Drive actions are enabled for your plan.",Partially feasible,Ingredient database + recipes must exist; write actions may depend on config; human verification for food safety/allergens. | Inputs: Recipe library; inventory on hand; suppliers; allergen flags.,“Menu -> ingredient BOM”: produce per-item BOM + consolidated purchase order.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Role-specific task sheets,"GPT can generate role-based checklists, shift briefs, and SOP snippets; can summarize ClickUp if connected.",Partially feasible,ClickUp “sync” documentation indicates read scope today; creating/updating tasks may not be available without write actions or external automation. | Inputs: Task templates; roles; event schema.,"“Checklist compiler”: output per role, per day, with dependencies and time estimates."
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Venue relationships strategically important,"GPT can draft outreach sequences, meeting agendas, and monthly check-in scripts; summarize venue notes if stored in docs.",Partially feasible,"Relationship system needs CRM writeback + reminders; tasks limited to 10 active tasks, so use batch cadence tasks not per-venue tasks. | Inputs: Venue database; contact list; last-touch dates; deal history.",“Relationship cadence”: generate next-touch plan + email drafts + logging payload.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Event planners matter too,Same as venues: communication templates and follow-ups.,Partially feasible,Automated sequence execution requires CRM/email automation tooling. | Inputs: Planner list; tone guidelines; prior messages.,“Planner follow-up”: draft + subject lines + versions by tone.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Venue ownership assigned to reps,GPT can generate owner dashboards and “overdue touchpoints”; produce accountability reports.,Partially feasible,Requires correct assignment data + reminders; Plus has limited governance for multi-user workflows. | Inputs: Assignment sheet; weekly cadence; success metrics.,“Owner report”: produce per-rep tasks next 7 days + context snippets.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Preferred vendors + location-aware recommending,GPT can recommend vendors based on an internal vendor list + event constraints; can supplement with web research.,Partially feasible,Needs curated vendor data; web results need verification; no guaranteed geo or pricing accuracy. | Inputs: Vendor list; service areas; pricing; SLAs; venue restrictions.,“Constraint satisfaction prompt”: explicit constraints + ranked recommendation table.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Tastings are a real sales mechanism,"GPT can generate tasting agendas, feedback forms, and conversion follow-up sequences; summarize tasting notes (including from record mode).",Partially feasible,Scheduling/booking is not fully automated; capture of evaluations must be structured. | Inputs: Tasting form; rubric; sample menus; pricing sheet.,“Tasting debrief”: extract objections + next steps + tailored proposal delta.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Commission tracking exists,GPT can compute commissions from a sheet and produce payout reports and exception flags.,Partially feasible,Needs consistent deal attribution; integration to payroll/accounting not included. | Inputs: Deal close export; commission rules; adjustments log.,“Commission engine prompt”: compute + audit trail + edge case list.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Google Sheets are everywhere,"Data analysis + sheet-friendly outputs (CSV, formulas, normalized tables) are a core strength.",Fully feasible,"Human still validates outputs; writeback depends on app actions configuration. | Inputs: Clean, normalized sheets; consistent headers.",“Sheet-first prompt”: output CSV/TSV + formulas + validation rules.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Google is main environment,Google Drive app/actions can unify Docs/Sheets/Slides actions; connected Google data has specific privacy rules.,Partially feasible,Workspace admin settings can affect actions; project-link sources may not support sync in-project. | Inputs: Drive folders per event; templates; shared permissions.,“Doc assembly prompt”: create proposal/BEO addendum from templates + variables.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Tool stack fragmentation,"GPT can reconcile “where truth lives,” propose consolidation, generate migration plans, and produce bridges (exports/import templates).",Partially feasible,Technical integration across tools is outside Plus-only; requires API/no-code automation layer. | Inputs: Inventory of systems + fields; sample exports from each.,“System mapping prompt”: produce canonical schema + mapping tables + migration steps.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Multiple departments (admin/sales/ops/etc.),"GPT can generate role-specific materials, but Plus is a personal plan-no enterprise workspace controls, RBAC, or “no training by default” posture like Business.",Partially feasible,Secure multi-user deployment is constrained; account sharing prohibited. | Inputs: If multi-user: individual accounts + shared templates; or move to Business plan.,“Role pack prompt”: produce a per-role playbook + standard prompts + QA checklist.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Structure should scale through people and systems,"GPT can produce scalable SOPs, training materials, and structured templates.",Partially feasible,"Systems scaling needs integrations, permissions, and auditability beyond Plus. | Inputs: SOP docs; training rubrics; standard outputs.",“Training module prompt”: create onboarding lessons + scenarios + rubrics.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Already a management layer,GPT can produce weekly exec briefs and KPI dashboards from sheets.,Partially feasible,KPI extraction depends on data consistency; deep research is not for ops KPI automation. | Inputs: KPI sheet; thresholds; definitions.,“Ops dashboard prompt”: compute KPIs + anomalies + actions.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Weekly rhythms,Scheduled Tasks can generate recurring briefs and reminders; limited to 10 active tasks; runs offline and notifies you.,Partially feasible,Tasks can’t use file uploads or GPTs; keep tasks lightweight and rely on stable text instructions. | Inputs: Fixed weekly prompt text; links to sheets/docs.,“Weekly cadence task”: stable prompt + explicit output format + escalation rules.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Recurring operational cycles,Same as weekly rhythms; plus can generate cycle checklists and seasonal playbooks.,Partially feasible,True operational orchestration requires event-driven automation outside Plus. | Inputs: Cycle definitions; triggers; owners.,“Cycle orchestrator prompt”: generate checklists + dependencies + owner routing.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Create order out of complexity,"GPT excels at converting messy notes into structured plans, checklists, and tables.",Partially feasible,Needs disciplined inputs; without structured data you’ll get drift. | Inputs: Standard intake forms; canonical schema.,"“Normalization-first”: force JSON schema, then generate artifacts."
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Social media mattered more,"GPT can generate content calendars, captions, hooks, and repurpose event stories; can generate images (as allowed), but marketing automation scheduling requires external tooling.",Partially feasible,"Approvals, brand safety, and scheduling still require separate systems. | Inputs: Brand guide; photo/video assets; event highlights.",“Content pipeline prompt”: brand constraints + 30-day calendar + variants.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,Strong candidate for templating,"Custom GPTs + Projects enable reusable templates for proposals, BEO summaries, prep lists, staffing plans.",Fully feasible,Governance/versioning across a team is better on Business. | Inputs: Template docs; naming conventions; QA checklists.,“Template pack”: instructions + few-shot examples + strict output schemas.
GPT Plus,Feature mapping,"Not broken, manually overextended",GPT Plus can reduce time spent drafting/formatting/reporting and improve consistency; but it won’t replace system integration.,Partially feasible,Bottlenecks that are “data movement” or “permissions/workflow routing” need tooling beyond Plus. | Inputs: Time study of tasks; pain-point ranking.,“Time-savings audit”: list top 20 tasks; propose GPT + template approach per task.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Overall business shape (structured recurring workflows),Projects + instructions + RAG; scheduled tasks,Partially supported,"Claude can centralize “how we run the week,” but Pro alone doesn’t unify data/permissions across tools into a true system of record.",Use Pro Projects to standardize SOPs now; for system-of-record automation add Claude API + DB.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Sales and lead management,Projects; connectors; custom connectors (remote MCP),Partially supported,No built-in Close CRM connector guaranteed; Pro can support work via custom connector + scheduled reporting.,Build/use a Close MCP connector (or automation platform) for lead ingest/routing; use API for event-driven triggers.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Lead source awareness and attribution,Projects; Drive/Sheets access; scheduled tasks,Partially supported,Strong for analytics and reporting if source data is accessible; less strong for automatic attribution without CRM integration.,Implement UTM/source normalization in CRM via API; use Pro for weekly insight writing.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Close CRM is important,Custom connectors via remote MCP,Partially supported,"Pro can connect to a remote MCP server you host; still not “native,” and automation depends on scheduled tasks + tool permissions.","Build MCP server that wraps Close API, with strict scopes and audit logging."
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Lead tracker used for cash forecasting,File creation + code execution; Drive/Sheets access,Partially supported,Claude can compute forecasts and generate updated spreadsheets; updating “live” Sheets reliably may require MCP/iPaaS.,Pro: generate forecast artifacts; Production: API + scheduled workflow + write-back to Sheets/DB.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Weekly labor reporting,File creation; connectors; scheduled tasks,Partially supported,Reporting is easy; automated labor ingestion depends on timekeeping/payroll system access.,Add HR/timekeeping integration via MCP or iPaaS; keep Claude Pro as reporting layer initially.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Scheduling is a major operational function,Google Calendar connector; scheduled tasks,Partially supported,"Calendar connector can create/update/delete events and find availability, but connector actions generally require approval; optimization logic is external.",Use Pro to draft schedules; for automated scheduling use solver + API + human approval gate.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Calendar is operational truth,Google Calendar connector,Partially supported,Connector provides strong read/write capability for events; but requires explicit approval in chat flows.,Use Claude Code cloud tasks for “daily calendar digest”; for real-time ops use API + event bus.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Jobs/events are operationally detailed,Projects; file creation; Drive connector,Fully supported,"Claude Pro is well-suited to creating structured “event briefs” from BEOs, notes, and timelines-especially in Projects.",Standardize an event-brief JSON/template; add MCP tools to store briefs in DB/CRM.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Visual readability for fast ops comprehension,File creation (spreadsheets); Projects,Partially supported,"Claude can generate formatted artifacts; but maintaining a living, color-coded ops sheet in Sheets is better via API/MCP tooling.",Implement a “one-click render” pipeline: DB->generated sheet/PDF->Drive.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Jobs vary in complexity,Projects; code execution,Fully supported,"Complexity scoring, staffing heuristics, and checklists are strong Claude tasks if you supply historic examples in a Project.",Add a complexity score field to event records; update automatically via API/MCP.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Reimbursements / expense handling,File creation; connectors,Partially supported,"Claude can parse receipts/invoices if provided as images, but Drive connector won’t process embedded images; accounting integration needed for automation.",Use specialized OCR + accounting API; Claude Pro can draft reimbursement summaries and approvals.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Weekly financial visibility,File creation + code execution; scheduled tasks,Partially supported,Forecasting and variance narration are strong; bank feeds/accounting ingestion not Pro-native.,Add Plaid/accounting platform + ETL; use Claude for variance explanations and executive weekly memo.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Shared food shopping / prep list,Projects; file creation; Drive/Sheets access,Fully supported,Claude can generate shopping and prep lists from menus + event counts; best results when recipes/yields live in Project knowledge.,Add inventory-on-hand + vendor catalog integration to turn lists into purchase orders.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Role-specific task sheets,Projects; connectors; custom connectors,Partially supported,Claude can generate role-based checklists; task-system write-back depends on ClickUp/PM integration.,"Build MCP tools: create_task, assign_task, checklist_template; add audit trail."
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Venue relationships are strategic,Projects; Drive/CRM connectors; email drafts,Partially supported,"Claude can draft outreach, meeting briefs, and follow-ups; Gmail connector cannot send emails.",Integrate email sending via CRM/marketing tool; use Claude Pro for personalization and sequences.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Event planners matter,Same as above,Partially supported,"Same constraints: writing is easy, sending/logging requires external system.",Add CRM logging + outreach automation and keep Claude as copy + research layer.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Venue ownership assigned to reps,Custom connectors; Projects,Partially supported,Ownership assignment is easy to decide; writing back to CRM requires integration.,Implement CRM field updates via MCP tool; add “approval required” safeguard.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Preferred vendors & location-aware recommendations,Projects + knowledge base,Fully supported,Claude is well-suited to recommendation generation if vendor list + geo coverage is stored in Project knowledge.,Put vendor DB in a real database; expose as MCP “search_vendor” tool.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Tastings (pipeline stage),Calendar connector; Projects; email drafts,Partially supported,Scheduling + brief generation works; closing workflow needs CRM integration and outbound messaging.,"Build tasting workflow: pre-brief, follow-up draft, deal stage update via MCP/API."
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Commission tracking,Code execution + file creation,Partially supported,Computation is easy; integration with payroll/accounting systems is not Pro-native.,Create “commission rules engine” in code; plug into payroll/accounting via API.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Google Sheets everywhere,Drive connector (Sheets as CSV); file creation,Partially supported,"Drive connector can pull Sheet data as CSV; for stable read/write at scale, use Sheets APIs via MCP or an add-on (requires API key).",Add Sheets API integration via MCP or iPaaS; reserve Pro for analysis + file generation.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Google is main environment,Connectors; scheduled tasks,Partially supported,Strong read/write for Calendar/Drive; but automation constraints remain.,Use Claude Code cloud tasks for daily/weekly loops; add API for event-triggered flows.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Fragmented tool stack,Custom connectors via remote MCP,Partially supported,"MCP can unify tools technically, but governance and reliability still require engineering.",Consolidate system-of-record decisions; implement integration layer (iPaaS/workflow engine).
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Multiple departments/roles,Projects (sharing only on Team/Enterprise),Not supported,"Pro is an individual consumer plan; org-wide sharing and admin controls are described for Team/Enterprise, not Pro.","Use Team/Enterprise for governance, or build your own RBAC + app using Claude API."
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Scale through people and systems,Scheduled tasks; Projects; connectors,Partially supported,Pro can reduce manual load but won’t substitute for a governed automation platform.,Use Pro for rapid SOP/template creation; move execution to API-backed workflows.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Management layer exists,Scheduled briefings; reports,Fully supported,"Weekly dashboards, standup summaries, and status briefs are excellent Pro use cases.",Implement “manager cockpit” outputs to Slack/Drive via scheduled tasks.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Weekly rhythm,Scheduled tasks,Fully supported,Claude Code cloud tasks can run weekly reports without your machine on.,Implement weekly schedule+labor+cash+revenue pack generation with standardized templates.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Recurring operational cycles,Scheduled tasks; Projects,Partially supported,Polling-based automation can cover many cycles; but event-driven tasks still need API/webhooks.,Add orchestration (Temporal/n8n) and Claude API to support event-driven triggers.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Creating order out of complexity,Projects; dashboards; file creation,Partially supported,Claude can generate simplified views; a durable “single pane of glass” needs a real data model and UI.,Build a canonical event schema + data pipeline; use Claude as reasoning layer.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Social media growth interest,Drafting + ideation,Fully supported,"Claude Pro is strong for content generation, repurposing, and strategy.",Add scheduling/analytics tool + content calendar; optionally integrate via MCP.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Candidate for templating (repeatable model),Projects; file generation,Partially supported,"Pro can create templates and SOP libraries, but productizing as a service needs API + packaging.",Use Pro to author templates; build a multi-tenant SaaS with Claude API for delivery.
Claude Pro,Requirement mapping,Manually overextended (need automation),Cowork/Claude Code; connectors,Partially supported,Pro reduces cognitive load but isn’t a substitute for an automation runtime with SLAs and governance.,Use Pro immediately for triage + templates; prioritize API-based automation for highest-load loops.
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Menu personalization engine,"Tailor menus to client preferences, budgets, dietary needs; auto-generate proposal variants",Priority: High | Complexity: Medium,Structured menu/recipe DB + cost model + CRM integration,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Event complexity scoring -> staffing recommendation,Turn event details into an estimated staffing plan and prep schedule,Priority: High | Complexity: Medium,Historic labeled examples + rules/optimization; write-back to schedule,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Inventory forecasting,Predict ingredient demand from upcoming events; reduce waste/stockouts,Priority: Medium | Complexity: High,Inventory DB + supplier catalogs + time series model,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Automated purchasing workflow,Convert prep list to vendor-specific purchase orders,Priority: Medium | Complexity: High,Vendor APIs/email ordering; approvals; accounting integration,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,OCR + invoice reconciliation,Extract invoice line-items; match to POs; flag discrepancies,Priority: High | Complexity: Medium,OCR service; accounting system integration,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Scheduling optimization,Optimize staff assignments across multiple events/locations; minimize travel time,Priority: High | Complexity: High,Optimization solver + constraints; write-back to calendar/task system,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Voice assistant for lead intake,"Answer calls, capture requirements, create a lead, schedule tasting",Priority: Medium | Complexity: High,Telephony + speech-to-text + CRM + guardrails,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Real-time kitchen monitoring summaries,Summarize IoT or prep-progress signals into actionable alerts,Priority: Low-Medium | Complexity: High,IoT stack + streaming pipeline; alert routing,
Claude Pro,Additional opportunities,Social content pipeline with performance feedback,"Generate content, schedule posting, and learn from analytics",Priority: Medium | Complexity: Medium,Social scheduler + analytics ingestion,

Catering & Events Business Automation Roadmap

Phase 0: Foundation
Systems Audit & Mapping
Environment Consolidation
Quick Win Automations

Phase 1: Sales Intelligence
Close CRM Optimization
Lead Source Attribution
Tasting Pipeline Automation
Cash Flow Forecasting
Commission Tracking

Phase 2: Execution Engine
Automated Weekly Job Board
Staffing & Labor Automation
Shopping & Prep Lists
Event Complexity Scoring
Expense Reimbursement

Phase 3: Relationship & Referrals
Venue Relationship CRM
Event Planner Management
ROI & Attribution Tracking
Preferred Vendor Network
Automated Nurture Sequences

Phase 4: Financial Command Center
Weekly Financial Dashboard
Event Profitability Tracking
Unified Command Center
Role-Based Task Lists

Phase 5: Marketing Engine
Content Capture System
Social Media Calendar
Event-to-Content Pipeline
Testimonial Automation
Marketing Analytics

Phase 6: Scale & Optimize
Automation Health Monitoring
Seasonal Scaling Playbooks
Data-Driven Process Optimization
Knowledge Base & SOPs
Template & Scale Assessment

Technology Stack
Core: Google Workspace, Close, ClickUp, Slack
Automation: Zapier, Make
Reporting: Looker Studio
Marketing: Buffer/Later
